affected_groups: Disadvantaged and Roma pupils facing consolidation
Rural families and unaccompanied minors relying on transport
Local schools and communities facing closure
Receiving consolidated schools absorbing displaced pupils
content.decision_question: Equity versus administrative efficiency: whether closing a school under the assumption that compensatory support (transport plus TEIP-style targeted help) fully substitutes for local provision is compatible with a genuine commitment to closing, not merely narrowing, the disadvantage gap
content.evidence_boundary: Partially settled: Portugal's 15-year TEIP record is repeatedly cited across voices as showing that compensatory measures narrow but do not close performance and dropout gaps — this pattern itself is treated as established. What remains contested and Hungary-specific is whether the same magnitude of gap-narrowing would occur here, given the scenario's own 'contested' evidence status and unvalidated Hungarian transport cost figures; no Hungary-specific cost-benefit or outcome data exists to confirm or refute transferability.
content.tension: Portugal's 15-year TEIP programme evidence shows that compensatory measures (transport plus targeted support) narrow but do not close the performance and dropout gap between disadvantaged and advantaged pupils, so treating closure as default with mitigation as an afterthought risks legitimizing an equity gap the policy claims to address.
content.title: Decision tension A14: S4
value_poles: administrative efficiency: whether closing a school under the assumption that compensatory support (transport plus TEIP-style targeted help) fully substitutes for local provision is compatible with a genuine commitment to closing, not merely narrowing, the disadvantage gap
